_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: The ability to communicate information and ideas in speaking so others will understand.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in preparing and delivering board-level presentations on data and AI strategy, translating technical complexity into business language appropriate for governance audiences.
comments: WIDAI LS-S-006 is a domain-specific expression of oral expression ability. Multiple WIDAI KSAs involve speaking (stakeholder engagement, team leadership, consultation). O*NET defines the general cognitive ability.
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: Moderate similarity (nist band: roughly as similar as dissimilar). The STS score reflects meaningful semantic overlap between the broader O*NET element and the narrower WIDAI KSA. The superset relationship is well-supported — the shared concepts are substantial enough for the model to detect cross-text alignment.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: F1 of 0.8697 indicates moderate token-level semantic alignment. Precision and Recall are balanced, indicating roughly symmetric semantic coverage between the two descriptions. The P > R pattern is atypical for a Superset relationship — it suggests the KSA may contain specificity that extends beyond the FDE's scope. This warrants attention during human review, though it may reflect domain-specific terminology in the KSA that the FDE covers conceptually without matching at the token level.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: Neutral-dominant (99.87%) — the model finds the descriptions related but neither implying nor contradicting each other. Neutral is common for Superset pairs where the scope gap is large — the FDE covers broader territory than the KSA, so the NLI model sees topical relatedness without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: The bi-encoder score (0.3165) is moderately lower than the STS cross-encoder (0.4768). The gap (0.16) indicates the relationship has a cross-abstraction component — some of the semantic alignment requires joint processing to detect, suggesting vocabulary or scope divergence between the two descriptions.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Low lexical overlap (6.06% of tokens shared). Some shared vocabulary exists (likely common functional words or broadly-used domain terms) but the bulk of each description uses distinct terminology.

signals.detail: STS 0.477 barely clears classification threshold for Superset of. This match is fragile and may indicate insufficient KSA coverage for this competency area.
